title: juncus tenuis subsp. anthelatus
intro: Juncus tenuis subsp. anthelatus, also known as jointed rush or joint rush, is a perennial plant species within the Juncus genus, characterized by its slender stems and small, green leaves. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which can help to soothe and heal wounds, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues. The health benefits of Juncus tenuis subsp. anthelatus include its ability to act as a natural expectorant, helping to clear mucus from the lungs and airways, and its potential to support the immune system. In herbal preparations, Juncus tenuis subsp. anthelatus is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also sometimes employed in traditional remedies for its supposed antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
